title: Aristolochia tithonusiana
intro: Aristolochia tithonusiana, also known as Tithonia Aristolochia or Mexican Birthwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Aristolochiaceae family. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aristolochia tithonusiana include aristolochic acids, which have been found to possess cytotoxic and anti-tumor properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aristolochia tithonusiana is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Aristolochia tithonusiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae. This family is commonly known as the birthwort or Dutchman's pipe family. Aristolochiaceae is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Members of the Aristolochiaceae family are known for their unique, often tubular flowers and are commonly found in woodland environments. The Aristolochiaceae family is a member of the larger order Piperales.

side_effects: Aristolochia tithonusiana contains aristolochic acid, which has been linked to potential side effects such as kidney damage and increased risk of certain types of cancer.
